header_summary:
Antoine expresses skepticism towards Pieter's proposal of using a tripwire mechanism to disable EC paths in Bitcoin, highlighting potential risks of miner collusion and the impracticality of assuming miners will act against their immediate economic interests. He critiques the strategy's reliance on soft forks and the game-theoretical soundness of the proposal, considering the possibility of economically rational miners bypassing security measures. This discussion can be further explored through the detailed source documentation [here](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/5f59804c-6a3b-41e7-9733-6c253353847an@googlegroups.com/T/#m702bc612e588067e2908595d9b70fd695e).

A new Bitcoin consensus rule proposal aims to establish a perpetual block subsidy while respecting the 21 million coin cap through the expiration of old UTXOs, transferring their value to a pending emission reserve. This plan, intended to extend miner incentives indefinitely without exceeding the supply limit, is open for community feedback and can be accessed for more details [here](https://github.com/bip-proposer/bitcoin-infinity/blob/main/infinity.md).

The integration of blockchain technology and AI is gaining traction, with significant academic and practical implications, particularly in secure transactions and contracts on platforms like Bitcoin. This intersection is detailed in a recent academic paper available [here](https://dl.acm.org/doi/pdf/10.1145/3372297.3417278), highlighting ongoing challenges and the potential for future innovations in cryptography and blockchain applications. The summary also touches on a new approach to handling Bitcoin transactions with an input-triggered expiry mechanism, enhancing security and operational efficiency, which is further discussed [here](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/input-triggered-transaction-expiry/2667).

recent_posts.title: Giving teeth to expected EC disabling: P2XX(-T)(-ML)
recent_posts.summary: - Antoine doubts the security of implementing tripwires in miner operations.  - He fears miners might collude, compromising the supposed long-term network security.  - Critical of over-relying on miners' economic sacrifices for network stability.

recent_posts.title: [BIP Proposal] Strict circular economy within the Bitcoin network
recent_posts.summary: - The proposal introduces a new rule for a perpetual Bitcoin block subsidy.  - Unused Bitcoin transaction outputs will expire after a set block limit.  - This plan ensures miner subsidies continue without surpassing the coin cap.

recent_posts.title: Accountable Computing On-Chain Contracts for AI Agents Supervision
recent_posts.summary:
- The Bitcoin blockchain's role in AI contract protocols is being actively explored.
- Real-world cryptography, especially homomorphic encryption, needs further refinement.
- Ongoing research focuses on enhancing blockchain for complex AI contracts securely.

recent_posts.title: Input-triggered transaction expiry
recent_posts.summary:
- This approach utilizes `nSequence` and `nLockTime` for transaction expiry.
- It enables more efficient HTLC forwarding without local-mempool monitoring.
- The method supports streamlined operations and reduces storage needs through `TEMPLATEHASH`.

recent_posts.title: Expiring HTLCs without free relay
recent_posts.summary:
- Proposed changes aim to enhance HTLC security by avoiding preimage publication reliance.
- Modifications include consensus on nSequence bit 21 and new opcodes to ensure secure transactions.
- These adjustments reduce vulnerability to network attacks and foster blockchain technology adoption.

recent_posts.title: Quantum Sunset Economics: a working paper analyzing PACT adoption
recent_posts.summary:
- The paper, now v2, includes reclassification of P2SH and P2WSH addresses.
- New findings report an increase in exposed Bitcoin from 25.30% to 35.30%.
- Section 6.5 discusses multisig's role against quantum attacks.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] P2P UTXO Set Sharing
active_posts.summary:
- The BIP introduces new methods for decentralized UTXO set sharing in Bitcoin's network.
- It includes new service bits and P2P messages to facilitate data transfer.
- The proposal aims to reduce new node setup time and increase network resilience.

active_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
active_posts.summary: - The proposal modifies BIP360 by enforcing one merkle path in P2MR control blocks.  - It aims to enhance privacy and security by disallowing depth-zero script trees.  - Feedback from P2TRv2 proponents is sought to assess the impact on UTXO fungibility.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary: - The proposal introduces a new rule for encoding specific 64-byte transactions in Bitcoin.  - It aims to prevent malleability issues by invalidating blocks with certain Merkle node preimages.  - The rule maintains transaction functionality while enhancing network security.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin privacy enhancement focuses on sidechains, L2s, and wallets, not on core protocol.
- Technologies like Confidential Transactions and CoinJoin optimize rather than transform privacy.
- Regulatory considerations and system complexities deter foundational privacy changes in Bitcoin.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts.summary:
- SegData proposes a new Bitcoin block data storage method emphasizing efficiency and optional retention.
- It modifies block serialization and extends the weight formula, incorporating prunable, unspendable reference outputs.
- SegData targets entities like indexers and timestamping services, but not data requiring script evaluations.

active_posts.title: Addressing the Diminishing Block Subsidy
active_posts.summary:
- A tail emission of 0.25 BTC per block will start in 2040 to stabilize mining income.
- Introducing tail emissions aims to balance network security costs between active and passive Bitcoin users.
- Critics fear that exceeding the 21 million BTC cap could harm Bitcoin's value reputation.

today_in_history_posts.title: BIP 151 use of HMAC_SHA512
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Ethan Heilman criticizes HMAC's necessity, advocating SHA256 for MAC security.
- Arthur Chen defends HMAC, highlighting its reliable security despite underlying hash function weaknesses.
- SHA256's use as a MAC is vulnerable due to its susceptibility to length extension attacks.

today_in_history_posts.title: [BOLT Draft] Onion Routing Spec
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Rusty Russell proposes restructuring the Lightning Network protocol.
- He suggests merging two components into a new 40-byte field for efficiency.
- Additional changes include log truncation and an updated replay protection scheme.

today_in_history_posts.title: Thoughts on scaling and consensus changes (2023)
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin's scalability involves creating 50,000 off-chain "bitcoin banks" for wide outreach.
- These entities will interconnect to provide liquidity and maintain affordability with security.
- Off-chain solutions focus on maintaining Bitcoin's value while ensuring scalable transactions.
